description: 8 The AllenScott Report By ROBERT S ALLEN and PAUL SCOTT R RTIR TH i WASHINGTONTheres a lot more behind President Kennedys pressure for reforms and personnel changes in the South Vietnam government than just an effort to end President Ngo Dinh Diems bloody dispute with the Buddhists Te President and his chief foreign policy advisers are using the internal disseasion in ith Vietnam as a cover for their efforts to oust Diem or strip him of control over military operations against the Communiz Viet Congs Since Diem under a plan prepared by his brot N7 Dinh Nhu began sending guerrillas into North Vietnam in June powerful forces within the administration have clamored for the President to curb the strong antiCommunist leader Headed by Dr Walt Rostow chairman of the State Depart ment Plaaning Council this group of advisers argued that Diems strategy of carrying the war into the privileged sanctuary of the Viet Congs in North Viet Nam could cause the fighting to escalate into a bigger war General Paul D Harkins head of the US Military Assistance Command in Saigon who favors the initiative by Diems forces violently disagreed with the views of the Rostow group but Presi
